Djokovic brings investor experience, but the on court proof point is still current: at 39, after reaching the Australian Open final earlier this year, Novak Djokovic remains in the mix at Wimbledon 2026, advancing to the third round after a four set win over Wu Yibing and a straight sets victory over Stefanos Tsitsipas.

Chairman and CEO Bill Ford said Djokovic’s discipline and long-term thinking align with General Atlantic’s culture as the firm looks to deepen ties between elite performance and its founder-focused investing approach."},{"url":"https://www.inc.com/amaya-nichole/novak-djokovic-next-big-move-on-wall-street/91366175","title":"Novak Djokovic’s Next Big Move Isn't on the Court—It’s on Wall Street","content":"The tennis legend is taking his first step into private equity.
“Novak’s discipline, tenacity, and drive have defined his career at the highest level of competition,” the chairman and CEO of General Atlantic Bill Ford said in a press release.
Notably, Djokovic already serves as a brand ambassador for Joe and the Juice, which General Atlantic has an investment in, making his new role a natural extension of a relationship already in place.

As Global Strategic Advisor, Mr. Djokovic will apply his experience as an athlete, investor, and entrepreneur, as General Atlantic continues to partner with founders and entrepreneurs building high-growth, category-defining businesses.
